title: FallbackController with error atom to HTTP status mapping

story.i_want_to: implement a FallbackController that maps Ecto/application error atoms and changesets to consistent JSON error responses with appropriate HTTP status codes
story.so_that: all API endpoints return uniform error payloads that AI agents can reliably parse, and controller actions can use idiomatic with/else pattern matching

rationale: A centralized FallbackController eliminates duplicated error-handling logic across every controller. Consistent error responses are critical for agent consumers who parse responses programmatically. The with-clause pattern (action_fallback) is the standard Phoenix approach and keeps controller actions clean and focused on the happy path.

acceptance_criteria.description: LoopctlWeb.FallbackController handles {:error, :not_found} and returns 404 with JSON body {"error": {"status": 404, "message": "Not found"}}
FallbackController handles {:error, :unauthorized} and returns 401 with JSON body {"error": {"status": 401, "message": "Unauthorized"}}
FallbackController handles {:error, :forbidden} and returns 403 with JSON body {"error": {"status": 403, "message": "Forbidden"}}

FallbackController handles {:error, %Ecto.Changeset{}} and returns 422 with JSON body containing structured validation errors in the format {"error": {"status": 422, "message": "Validation failed", "details": {"field": ["error message"]}}}
FallbackController handles {:error, :conflict} and returns 409 with appropriate message
FallbackController handles {:error, :rate_limited} and returns 429 with appropriate message
FallbackController handles {:error, :unprocessable_entity, message} (3-tuple with custom message) and returns 422 with the provided message
An ErrorJSON view module renders all error responses as JSON (no HTML error pages)
Unhandled errors (500) return a generic {"error": {"status": 500, "message": "Internal server error"}} without leaking stack traces or internal details

test_cases.name: FallbackController renders 404 for :not_found
expected_results: Response body is JSON with error.status = 404 and error.message = "Not found"

test_cases.name: FallbackController renders 422 with changeset errors
preconditions: An invalid Ecto.Changeset is created with errors on :name and :email
steps: Create changeset with errors: %Ecto.Changeset{errors: [name: {"can't be blank", []}, email: {"is invalid", []}], valid?: false}
expected_results: Response body JSON has error.details.name containing "can't be blank"
Response body JSON has error.details.email containing "is invalid"

test_cases.name: FallbackController renders 429 for :rate_limited
expected_results: Response body JSON has error.message = "Too many requests"

test_cases.name: FallbackController renders custom message for 3-tuple
steps: Call FallbackController.call(conn, {:error, :unprocessable_entity, "Cycle detected in dependency graph"})
expected_results: Response body JSON has error.message = "Cycle detected in dependency graph"

test_cases.name: 500 errors do not leak internal details
preconditions: A test controller action that raises an exception exists
steps: Send a request to the test endpoint that raises RuntimeError
expected_results: Response body JSON has error.message = "Internal server error"
No stack trace or internal module names appear in the response

technical_notes: Use action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController in a base controller or individual controllers
Changeset error traversal: Ecto.Changeset.traverse_errors(changeset, fn {msg, opts} -> ... end) to produce human-readable messages
Consider a LoopctlWeb.ErrorHelpers module for changeset → map translation that can be reused
The ErrorJSON view should handle the render/2 callback for all error templates (404.json, 422.json, 500.json, etc.)
In production, ensure Phoenix.Endpoint error handler uses ErrorJSON, not ErrorHTML
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
